Title: Two Sum
Description: Find two numbers in an array whose sum equals a target value.
Answer: Use a HashMap to store previously seen numbers and check whether the complement exists while iterating through the array.
InterviewTip: Explain the brute-force O(n²) solution first before optimizing with a HashMap.

Title: Valid Anagram
Description: Determine whether two strings are anagrams of each other.
Answer: Count character frequencies using a HashMap or sort both strings and compare them.
InterviewTip: Mention both sorting and frequency-count approaches.

Title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
Description: Find the length of the longest substring without duplicate characters.
Answer: Use the Sliding Window technique with a HashMap to track the latest index of each character.
InterviewTip: Explain why the window moves instead of restarting.

Title: Product of Array Except Self
Description: Return an array where each element equals the product of all other elements.
Answer: Compute prefix and suffix products without using division.
InterviewTip: Mention why division is not allowed.

Title: Binary Search
Description: Search for a target element in a sorted array.
Answer: Repeatedly divide the search space into halves until the target is found.
InterviewTip: Always mention the array must be sorted.

Title: Reverse Linked List
Description: Reverse a singly linked list.
Answer: Iteratively reverse pointers using previous current and next pointers.
InterviewTip: Explain both iterative and recursive approaches.

Title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
Description: Find the maximum depth of a binary tree.
Answer: Use recursive DFS or iterative BFS to calculate tree depth.
InterviewTip: Explain why DFS naturally computes height.

Title: Number of Islands
Description: Count the number of islands in a grid.
Answer: Use DFS or BFS to traverse connected land cells and mark them visited.
InterviewTip: Discuss both DFS and BFS solutions.

Title: Climbing Stairs
Description: Count the number of distinct ways to reach the top of a staircase.
Answer: Recognize the Fibonacci pattern and use dynamic programming.

Title: Group Anagrams
Description: Group words that are anagrams of each other.
Answer: Use sorted strings or character frequency as the HashMap key.
InterviewTip: Explain why identical sorted strings belong to the same group.

Title: Min Stack
Description: Design a stack that supports retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
Answer: Maintain an additional stack that keeps track of the minimum value.
InterviewTip: Mention why an auxiliary stack is needed.

Title: Implement Queue using Stacks
Description: Implement a queue using two stacks.
Answer: Use one stack for enqueue and another for dequeue operations.

Title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
Description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
Answer: Use a min heap of size k or QuickSelect.
InterviewTip: Mention both Heap and QuickSelect approaches.

Title: Implement Trie
Description: Design a Trie supporting insert search and startsWith.
Answer: Store characters in Trie nodes with child pointers.
InterviewTip: Explain why Trie is efficient for prefix searches.

Title: Jump Game
Description: Determine whether you can reach the last index.
Answer: Track the farthest reachable index while traversing the array.

Title: Fibonacci Number
Description: Return the nth Fibonacci number.
Answer: Use recursion with memoization or iterative dynamic programming.
InterviewTip: Mention why naive recursion is exponential.

Title: Single Number
Description: Find the element that appears only once.
Answer: Use XOR to cancel duplicate values.

Title: Second Highest Salary
Description: Write an SQL query to find the second highest salary.
Answer: Use ORDER BY with LIMIT/OFFSET or a subquery using MAX.

Title: Factory Design Pattern
Description: Explain the Factory Design Pattern.
Answer: Describe how object creation is delegated to subclasses or factory methods.

Title: Design YouTube
Description: Design a scalable video streaming platform similar to YouTube.
Answer: Discuss storage encoding CDN load balancing caching and recommendation architecture.

Title: Tell me about yourself
Description: Introduce yourself professionally.
Answer: Start with your education then discuss relevant projects internship technical skills achievements and conclude with why you are excited to join Google.
InterviewTip: Keep your answer under two minutes and tailor it to the Software Engineer role.

Title: Why do you want to work at Google?
Description: Explain your motivation for joining Google.
Answer: Discuss Google's innovation engineering culture learning opportunities global impact and how your skills align with its mission.
InterviewTip: Do not say salary is the main reason.

Title: What are your strengths?
Description: Describe your biggest strengths.
Answer: Highlight problem solving adaptability continuous learning teamwork and communication with real examples.
InterviewTip: Support every strength with an example.

Title: What is your biggest weakness?
Description: Discuss a genuine weakness.
Answer: Mention a real weakness explain how you recognized it and what you are doing to improve it.
InterviewTip: Never say you have no weaknesses.

Title: Describe yourself in three words.
Description: Choose three words that best describe your personality.
Answer: Select positive qualities like Curious Adaptable and Responsible and justify each with an example.

Title: Tell me about a time you showed leadership.
Description: Describe a leadership experience.
Answer: Use the STAR method to explain how you motivated a team solved a problem and achieved success.
InterviewTip: Always structure behavioral answers using STAR.

Title: Describe a challenging team project.
Description: Explain how you handled teamwork challenges.
Answer: Discuss communication collaboration conflict resolution and successful delivery.
InterviewTip: Focus on your contribution rather than blaming teammates.

Title: How do you handle constructive criticism?
Description: Explain your approach to feedback.
Answer: Describe how you actively listen accept feedback improve yourself and apply lessons learned.

Title: Where do you see yourself in five years?
Description: Discuss your career goals.
Answer: Talk about becoming a skilled engineer taking ownership of impactful projects mentoring others and continuous learning.
InterviewTip: Align your goals with Google's growth opportunities.

Title: Why should we hire you?
Description: Explain why you are the right candidate.
Answer: Combine your technical skills internship experience problem solving mindset and enthusiasm for learning into one compelling answer.
InterviewTip: End with confidence but avoid sounding arrogant.

Title: Tell me about a time you led a team
Description: Describe a situation where you took leadership responsibility.
Answer: Use the STAR method to explain the situation your actions and the successful outcome.
InterviewTip: Focus on measurable impact and teamwork.

Title: Describe a time you worked with a difficult teammate
Description: Explain how you handled conflict within your team.
Answer: Discuss active listening empathy communication and collaboration to resolve the issue.

Title: Tell me about a disagreement you had with your manager
Description: Describe a professional disagreement.
Answer: Explain how you respectfully presented your viewpoint listened to feedback and reached a positive outcome.

Title: Tell me about a time you made a mistake
Description: Describe a mistake and what you learned from it.
Answer: Accept responsibility explain how you fixed the issue and describe what you learned.
InterviewTip: Choose a real mistake with a positive ending.

Title: Describe a situation where you motivated your team
Description: Explain how you inspired others to achieve a goal.
Answer: Discuss how you communicated the vision encouraged collaboration and achieved success.

Title: Tell me about a successful team project
Description: Describe a project completed successfully by your team.
Answer: Explain your role challenges faced collaboration and final outcome using the STAR framework.

Title: Describe a time when you had to explain a technical concept to a non-technical person
Description: Explain how you simplified a complex topic.
Answer: Use simple language real-world examples and confirm understanding.

Title: Tell me about a time you handled multiple deadlines
Description: Describe how you managed competing priorities.
Answer: Explain how you prioritized tasks communicated expectations and successfully delivered results.

Title: Describe a time when you received negative feedback
Description: Explain how you responded to constructive criticism.
Answer: Show that you accepted feedback improved your performance and appreciated the learning opportunity.

Title: Tell me about a time you failed
Description: Describe a significant failure in your career or academics.
Answer: Explain the failure what you learned how you improved and how the experience helped you grow.
InterviewTip: Always finish with the lesson learned.

Title: Train Crossing Problem
Description: Solve a train crossing aptitude question.
Answer: Calculate speed distance and time using the appropriate formulas.

Title: Profit and Loss
Description: Calculate profit percentage from a business scenario.
Answer: Use Profit% = Profit/Cost Price × 100.

Title: Probability Basics
Description: Find the probability of selecting a specific object from a group.
Answer: Use favorable outcomes divided by total outcomes.

Title: Time and Work
Description: Calculate the time required for multiple people working together.
Answer: Use work efficiency concepts and LCM methods.

Title: Blood Relations
Description: Determine family relationships from given information.
Answer: Draw a family tree to simplify the problem.

Title: Seating Arrangement
Description: Arrange people according to given conditions.
Answer: Use diagrams to eliminate incorrect possibilities.

Title: Number Series
Description: Find the missing number in a sequence.
Answer: Identify arithmetic geometric or mixed patterns.

Title: Synonyms and Antonyms
Description: Choose the correct synonym or antonym of a word.
Answer: Understand vocabulary and contextual meaning.

Title: Email Writing
Description: Write a professional email responding to a client.
Answer: Use a clear subject greeting concise message and professional closing.

Title: Presentation Skills
Description: How would you present a technical project to senior management?
Answer: Focus on business impact architecture key achievements challenges and results using simple language.
